pages.text:
CharCoat CTI can save you money on heating or cooling costs, reduce your maintenance frequency and
keep your team safer.
COST REDUCTION
With the insulation properties of CTI heat loss (hot or cold) is reduced significantly leading to a more
efficient and cost‐effective business unit.
By retaining the temperature, the energy required to produce the temperature for the process is lessened
saving dollars, or it can be by not having to run heating or cooling units as frequently saving power with
the likes of Air Conditioner running costs.
An example of cost savings was realized at a fuel production plant in Russia where the boiler efficiency
was lower than desired costing additional fuel to run the unit. The average temperature of the boiler outer
surface design was meant to be 60oC while running, this was calculated to give a heat efficiency loss of 2%
to 5%. On taken temperature readings over the boiler unit it was found the surface area exceeded 100oC
(due to degrading of boiler internal lining) so the fuel efficiency loss was much greater than first thought.
The decision was made to do an application of CTI as an ultra‐thin thermal coating. The calculations for
savings were a reduction of 3.7% on fuel costs to run the boiler. The CTI was applied at the end September
2016, from the first five months of operation the fuel consumption was recorded and compared to the
same calendar month from the previous year (consumption varies with the seasons). The average
reduction over the five‐month period was 5.5% almost 50% higher than had been calculated, with this
payback was <7mths.
Fig. 1 Boiler Fuel Consumption Reduction Figures
FUEL CONSUMPTION (TUT)
MONTH % OF REDUCTION
October 7.17 %
November 6.18 %
December 5.88 %
January 4.90 %
February 3.46 %
From this first study after the five months of operation it confirmed that it was the right decision to apply
the CTI as an Ultra‐Thin Thermal Insulation, other benefits included:
 Reduced gas consumption.
 Stabilized the temperature inside the furnace.
 Improved quality of the resulting product.
 Improved safety of staff (furnace temp. not exceeding 60°C).
 Facilitated external monitoring of the state of the furnace body. In the case of the
temperature of the body there were observed “red stains. “ POK” is almost transparent
to monitor the surface of thermal imaging equipment.
 The noise level from the operation of the process furnace has decreased.

pages.text:
An uninsulated hot water tank is another example of the benefits of using CTI as a thermal insulation. This
tank had an average surface temperature of 51oC. CTI was applied at <1mm coating over the entire hot
water tank.
This resulted in a reducing the temperature to 39.5oC degrees on the surface of the tank, meaning the
energy requirement will be reduced to maintain and produce the hot water. Measurements of heat flow
strength (IPP‐2) showed 140 w/m2, compared to the initial figures of 370 W/m2 on min. vat insulation.
Prior to coating temp ave. 51oC After coating with <1mm of CTI
Making a calculation for a standard tank according to SNIP 2.04. 14‐88
Original Data
 Energy ‐ water
 Temperature of the coolant is 60oC
 Ambient air temperature is 25oC
 Tank diameter
 Tank height
Calculation using CTI as a energy efficient insulation layer:
Calculation using mineral cotton wool as an insulation layer:

pages.text:
COMPARISON ON APPLICATION METHODS
CTI
 Clean tank
 Apply CTI with brush, roller or spray
 Clean up
Mineral cotton wool
 Anti‐corrosion treatment of the tank
 Installation of bandage fasteners ‐ 2 layers
 Installation of mineral wool
 Waterproofing mineral wool
 Montage bandage
 Installation of the cover layer
 The final coloration of the tank in 2 layers
 Guarantee life of thermal insulation from mineral wool is 5 years (According to the site personnel
well before the 5 years the material dissolved and crumbled down the structure).
 Replacing the insulation requires work to disassemble the structure, replace the elements and
re‐insulate.
Conclusions
 CTI material has an excellent adhesion to the metal surface.
 Reducing heat loss and saving energy resources.
 Significant advantage over the traditional insulation and the speed of application of CTI.
 The work was carried out without stopping and disabling the equipment.
 It is easily applied to the surface of any shape.
 Does not create an additional load on the design.
 The use of CTI liquid insulation allows for significant work in comparison with traditional insulation
work, as well as to eliminate the possibility of building waste, which is in the process of application
of traditional insulators.
 The material reduces the time for location and emergency management.
 The treated surface has an aesthetic look.
 Long life of the detention facility, up to 10 years.

pages.text:
MAINTENANCE
Traditional insulation and cladding protection to both retain temperature and to protect any workers from
burns has always suffered from the issue of CUI (Corrosion Under Insulation). When you change to CTI
then CUI is gone and is no longer an issue.
CUI can take place under any insulation.
Corrosion under insulation (CUI) is the corrosion of piping and vessels that occurs beneath insulation
because of water penetration. The water can come from rainwater, leakage, deluge system water, wash
water, or sweating from temperature cycling or low temperature operation such as refrigeration units.
For corrosion to occur, the carbon steel must be directly contacted by aerated water and may be
accelerated by other factors such as temperature or other contaminants. Water enters an insulated
system primarily through breaks in the weatherproofing from sources such as:
 Rainfall
 Drift from cooling towers.
 Spray from wash downs.
 Condensation on cold surface after vapour barrier.
 Damage (when the temperature of the steel surface is lower than the atmospheric dew point).
One of the biggest issues with CUI is that it is occurring hidden under the traditional insulation, this can
result in not finding the problem until it shuts your operation down costing lots of $$ in lost production
even without the maintenance repair bill.
Stopping CUI from starting at all can be a simple choice of product. Typically pipe insulation has been
lagging and encased in sheeting. So many joins and leak points create a perfect scenario for CUI. CharCoat
CTI, CTI‐120, CTI‐300 and CTI‐600 are built for purpose in insulating pipe and substrate, lowering
temperature to stop epidermal burn and of course eradicate CUI once and for all.
CTI if damaged by operations or hit by machines is simple to patch with a brush and small pot of CTI and
can generally be applied while the equipment is running. If maintenance costs and downtime are issues
for you then CTI is the way to improve you operation and reduce costs and maintenance frequency.
Monitoring your maintenance costs along with knowing what lost production can cost you is an important
part of improve your production stream and being able to increase output while lowering your cost/unit.
A simple example is production rate times lost time times production unit loss, i.e. and example below for
say an iron ore mining operation would be:
Feed rate X Feed Grade X minutes lost X recovery i.e. 2000tph @ 2% HG @ 98.5% recovery, with a Fe price
of US$93/t and the plant was stopped because of a breakdown for 2.5hrs it equates to a loss revenue
(opportunity) of US$104K.

pages.text:
SAFETY
Anyone who has worked in heavy industry over the last 20+ years will know how much we have improved
in the area of safety and looking after our most important asset, our workers. If an accident occurs where
someone is hurt or injured there is a lot of time, money spent on sorting along with the reputational
damage that can cause to you company’s name and image.
CTI can reduce the risk of epidermal burns hugely for both hot and cold pipelines and other plant
equipment. It will also reduce the temperature of working areas when applied correctly to building rooves
and surfaces, increasing employee comfort and productivity.
By applying CTI in any areas where personnel can come in contact with high or very low temperature pipes
or other plant items you can ensure (DFT dependent on what temperature the pipe or unit is running).
The photo below shows the temperature reading of a pipe at 130.4oC before it had CTI coating applied.
The 2nd photo post coating shows a temperature of 39.3oC which gives you the knowledge and comfort
that your workers are not at risk of epidermal burns in that area.
